title: 「By contrast」轉折句
titleEn: Using By contrast to introduce a clear contrast

omissions: English translations for Chinese-only Important Rules and Benefits explanations are editorial; English content already present in the PDF is retained.
The PDF supplies the descriptive lesson title in Chinese only; titleEn is an editorial translation.

formula: Statement 1. By contrast, + complete contrasting statement.

formulas.formula: Statement 1. By contrast, + complete contrasting statement.

example: Some customs are public and visible. By contrast, deeper values are often hidden in daily habits.
exampleZh: 有些習俗是公開而明顯的。相比之下，更深層的價值往往隱藏在日常習慣之中。

examples.en: Some customs are public and visible. By contrast, deeper values are often hidden in daily habits.
examples.zh: 有些習俗是公開而明顯的。相比之下，更深層的價值往往隱藏在日常習慣之中。
examples.highlight: By contrast, deeper values are often hidden in daily habits.

benefits.en: It makes the difference immediately clear, so readers do not have to infer the relationship between the two sentences.
benefits.zh: 讓分別一目了然；讀者不需要自行猜測兩個句子的關係。

benefits.en: It helps you organise comparisons by presenting one person, method or situation before introducing the other.
benefits.zh: 幫助你有條理地作出比較：先介紹第一個人物、方法或情況，再介紹另一個情況。

benefits.en: It gives the second idea more attention and draws the reader towards the difference.
benefits.zh: 突出第二項資料，把讀者的注意力帶到第二項資料及兩者之間的差異。

benefits.en: It gives you an alternative to but and adds variety to your sentence structures.
benefits.zh: 避免反覆使用 but，提供另一種表達對比的方法，令句式更有變化。

benefits.en: It is useful in explanations, essays and reports when comparing viewpoints, measures, findings, people, places or social phenomena.
benefits.zh: 適合用於解釋、作文和報告，尤其是比較不同觀點、措施、研究結果、人物、地方或社會現象。

benefits.en: It provides a useful speaking frame: “One person or situation is... By contrast, the other is...”
benefits.zh: 提供實用的口語句子框架：「One person or situation is... By contrast, the other is...」

rules.en: By contrast usually appears at the beginning of the second sentence.
rules.zh: By contrast 通常放在第二個句子的開頭。
examples: The library is quiet. By contrast, the café is noisy.

rules.en: A comma must follow By contrast.
rules.zh: By contrast 後面必須加逗號。
examples: Correct: By contrast, the second method is faster.
Incorrect: By contrast the second method is faster.

rules.en: By contrast must be followed by a complete sentence containing a subject and a verb.
rules.zh: By contrast 後面必須接完整句子；完整句子需要包含主語和動詞。
examples: Correct: By contrast, the new machine works quietly.
Incorrect: By contrast, the new machine.

rules.en: The two sentences must compare related people, places, methods, periods, groups, or the same thing in different situations.
rules.zh: 兩個句子必須比較相關的事物，例如兩個人、兩個地方、兩種方法、兩段時間、兩個群體，或同一事物在不同情況下的表現。

rules.en: By contrast is not used to express cause or result; its main purpose is to identify a clear difference between two pieces of information.
rules.zh: By contrast 不是用來表達原因或結果；它的主要作用是指出兩項資料之間的明顯分別。

rules.en: But normally joins two parts within one sentence, whereas By contrast usually begins the second sentence.
rules.zh: By contrast 和 but 的句子結構不同：but 通常在同一個句子內連接兩個部分；By contrast 通常放在第二個句子的開頭。
examples: The old system is slow, but the new system is fast.
The old system is slow. By contrast, the new system is fast.

sourceOmissions: English translations for Chinese-only Important Rules and Benefits explanations are editorial; English content already present in the PDF is retained.
The PDF supplies the descriptive lesson title in Chinese only; titleEn is an editorial translation.

en: Rewrite each sentence or pair of sentences using By contrast to show a clear difference. Keep the first idea first and begin the second sentence with By contrast.
The first word of each answer has been provided.
zh: 使用 By contrast 改寫以下句子，以表達兩項資料之間的明顯分別。請保留第一項資料的次序，並以 By contrast 開始第二個句子。
每題已提供答案的第一個字。

questions.prompt: The library is quiet, but the café next door is noisy.
questions.promptZh: 圖書館很安靜，但隔壁的咖啡店很嘈吵。
questions.answer: The library is quiet. By contrast, the café next door is noisy.
questions.answerZh: 圖書館很安靜。相比之下，隔壁的咖啡店很嘈吵。
questions.highlight: The library is quiet. By contrast, the café next door is noisy.

questions.prompt: Amy enjoys reading indoors, while Leo prefers playing outside.
questions.promptZh: 艾米喜歡在室內閱讀，而里奧較喜歡在戶外玩耍。
questions.answer: Amy enjoys reading indoors. By contrast, Leo prefers playing outside.
questions.answerZh: 艾米喜歡在室內閱讀。相比之下，里奧較喜歡在戶外玩耍。
questions.highlight: Amy enjoys reading indoors. By contrast, Leo prefers playing outside.

questions.prompt: This bus stops at every station, but the express train stops only twice.
questions.promptZh: 這輛巴士每個車站都會停，而特快列車只停兩次。
questions.answer: This bus stops at every station. By contrast, the express train stops only twice.
questions.answerZh: 這輛巴士每個車站都會停。相比之下，特快列車只停兩次。
questions.highlight: This bus stops at every station. By contrast, the express train stops only twice.

questions.prompt: My old phone is slow, whereas my new phone works quickly.
questions.promptZh: 我的舊手提電話很慢，而我的新手提電話運作得很快。
questions.answer: My old phone is slow. By contrast, my new phone works quickly.
questions.answerZh: 我的舊手提電話很慢。相比之下，我的新手提電話運作得很快。
questions.highlight: My old phone is slow. By contrast, my new phone works quickly.

questions.answer: The front room is bright. By contrast, the back room is dark.

questions.prompt: School lunches are usually cheap. Restaurant meals, in comparison, are expensive.

questions.answer: School lunches are usually cheap. By contrast, restaurant meals are expensive.
questions.answerZh: 學校午餐通常很便宜。相比之下，餐廳膳食則較昂貴。
questions.highlight: School lunches are usually cheap. By contrast, restaurant meals are expensive.

questions.prompt: Nina speaks softly, unlike her brother, who speaks loudly.

questions.highlight: Nina speaks softly. By contrast, her brother speaks loudly.

questions.answer: Winter days are short. By contrast, summer days are long.

questions.answer: The blue bag is light. By contrast, the black suitcase is heavy.

questions.prompt: Our morning class has only twelve students, whereas the afternoon class has thirty students.

questions.prompt: Students who revise regularly usually feel prepared. However, those who study only on the night before an exam often feel anxious.
questions.promptZh: 定期溫習的學生通常會感到準備充足。然而，只在考試前一晚溫習的學生往往會感到焦慮。

questions.prompt: The eastern route is shorter but steeper, whereas the western route is longer but easier to walk.
questions.promptZh: 東面路線較短但較斜，而西面路線較長但較容易步行。
questions.answer: The eastern route is shorter but steeper. By contrast, the western route is longer but easier to walk.
questions.answerZh: 東面路線較短但較斜。相比之下，西面路線較長但較容易步行。

questions.prompt: The company used to train new staff in person. It now, however, provides most training through an online platform.
questions.promptZh: 公司以往會親自培訓新職員。然而，現在大部分培訓都透過網上平台進行。
questions.answer: The company used to train new staff in person. By contrast, it now provides most training through an online platform.

questions.prompt: One charity spends most of its money on emergency aid, while the other invests mainly in long-term education projects.
questions.promptZh: 一間慈善機構把大部分資金用於緊急援助，而另一間則主要投資於長期教育項目。

questions.prompt: The first proposal would reduce costs immediately. On the other hand, the second might produce greater benefits over time.
questions.promptZh: 第一項建議可以立即降低成本。另一方面，第二項建議長遠可能帶來更大的效益。
questions.answer: The first proposal would reduce costs immediately. By contrast, the second might produce greater benefits over time.
questions.answerZh: 第一項建議可以立即降低成本。相比之下，第二項建議長遠可能帶來更大的效益。
questions.highlight: The first proposal would reduce costs immediately. By contrast, the second might produce greater benefits over time.

questions.prompt: In crowded areas, shared bicycles can save space, whereas private cars require much more room for parking.
questions.promptZh: 在人口密集的地區，共享單車可以節省空間，而私家車需要更多泊車位置。
questions.answer: In crowded areas, shared bicycles can save space. By contrast, private cars require much more room for parking.
questions.answerZh: 在人口密集的地區，共享單車可以節省空間。相比之下，私家車需要更多泊車位置。
questions.highlight: In crowded areas, shared bicycles can save space. By contrast, private cars require much more room for parking.

questions.prompt: Some cultural customs are easy for visitors to notice, whereas the values behind them may remain hidden in everyday behaviour.
questions.promptZh: 部分文化習俗很容易被訪客察覺，而背後的價值觀可能隱藏在日常行為之中。
questions.answer: Some cultural customs are easy for visitors to notice. By contrast, the values behind them may remain hidden in everyday behaviour.
questions.answerZh: 部分文化習俗很容易被訪客察覺。相比之下，背後的價值觀可能隱藏在日常行為之中。
questions.highlight: Some cultural customs are easy for visitors to notice. By contrast, the values behind them may remain hidden in everyday behaviour.

questions.prompt: A face-to-face meeting allows people to notice tone and body language, while an email gives them more time to organise a careful response.
questions.promptZh: 面對面會議讓人留意語氣和身體語言，而電郵則給予他們更多時間組織審慎的回應。
questions.answer: A face-to-face meeting allows people to notice tone and body language. By contrast, an email gives them more time to organise a careful response.
questions.answerZh: 面對面會議讓人留意語氣和身體語言。相比之下，電郵則給予他們更多時間組織審慎的回應。
questions.highlight: A face-to-face meeting allows people to notice tone and body language. By contrast, an email gives them more time to organise a careful response.

questions.prompt: The city invested heavily in building new roads. The neighbouring region, however, focused on improving buses and railway services.
questions.promptZh: 該城市投放大量資源興建新道路。然而，鄰近地區則集中改善巴士和鐵路服務。
questions.answer: The city invested heavily in building new roads. By contrast, the neighbouring region focused on improving buses and railway services.
questions.answerZh: 該城市投放大量資源興建新道路。相比之下，鄰近地區則集中改善巴士和鐵路服務。
questions.highlight: The city invested heavily in building new roads. By contrast, the neighbouring region focused on improving buses and railway services.

questions.prompt: Older machines were designed to perform one fixed task, whereas modern devices can often be updated to carry out several different functions.
questions.promptZh: 舊式機器被設計成執行一項固定任務，而現代裝置通常可以更新，以執行多種不同功能。
questions.answer: Older machines were designed to perform one fixed task. By contrast, modern devices can often be updated to carry out several different functions.
questions.answerZh: 舊式機器被設計成執行一項固定任務。相比之下，現代裝置通常可以更新，以執行多種不同功能。
questions.highlight: Older machines were designed to perform one fixed task. By contrast, modern devices can often be updated to carry out several different functions.

questions.prompt: When demand is low, customers may receive discounts, but when demand rises sharply, prices often increase.
questions.promptZh: 當需求較低時，顧客可能獲得折扣；但當需求急劇上升時，價格通常會增加。
questions.answer: When demand is low, customers may receive discounts. By contrast, when demand rises sharply, prices often increase.
questions.answerZh: 當需求較低時，顧客可能獲得折扣。相比之下，當需求急劇上升時，價格通常會增加。
questions.highlight: When demand is low, customers may receive discounts. By contrast, when demand rises sharply, prices often increase.

questions.prompt: The first research group studied how the disease spreads, while the second examined how patients recover after treatment.
questions.promptZh: 第一個研究小組研究疾病如何傳播，而第二個小組則研究病人在接受治療後如何康復。
questions.answer: The first research group studied how the disease spreads. By contrast, the second examined how patients recover after treatment.
questions.answerZh: 第一個研究小組研究疾病如何傳播。相比之下，第二個小組則研究病人在接受治療後如何康復。
questions.highlight: The first research group studied how the disease spreads. By contrast, the second examined how patients recover after treatment.

questions.prompt: Schools in wealthy districts may have access to modern equipment, whereas schools in poorer areas sometimes struggle to replace basic classroom materials.
questions.promptZh: 富裕地區的學校可能擁有現代化設備，而較貧困地區的學校有時連更換基本課堂物資也有困難。
questions.answer: Schools in wealthy districts may have access to modern equipment. By contrast, schools in poorer areas sometimes struggle to replace basic classroom materials.
questions.answerZh: 富裕地區的學校可能擁有現代化設備。相比之下，較貧困地區的學校有時連更換基本課堂物資也有困難。
questions.highlight: Schools in wealthy districts may have access to modern equipment. By contrast, schools in poorer areas sometimes struggle to replace basic classroom materials.

questions.prompt: The short-term plan aims to solve the most urgent problems, but the long-term strategy is intended to prevent similar problems from returning.
questions.promptZh: 短期計劃旨在解決最迫切的問題，但長期策略則旨在防止類似問題再次出現。
questions.answer: The short-term plan aims to solve the most urgent problems. By contrast, the long-term strategy is intended to prevent similar problems from returning.
questions.answerZh: 短期計劃旨在解決最迫切的問題。相比之下，長期策略則旨在防止類似問題再次出現。
questions.highlight: The short-term plan aims to solve the most urgent problems. By contrast, the long-term strategy is intended to prevent similar problems from returning.

questions.prompt: Some leaders make decisions after consulting the whole team, while others prefer to act quickly and accept personal responsibility for the result.
questions.promptZh: 部分領袖會在諮詢整個團隊後作出決定，而其他領袖則較喜歡迅速行動，並親自承擔結果的責任。
questions.answer: Some leaders make decisions after consulting the whole team. By contrast, others prefer to act quickly and accept personal responsibility for the result.
questions.answerZh: 部分領袖會在諮詢整個團隊後作出決定。相比之下，其他領袖則較喜歡迅速行動，並親自承擔結果的責任。
questions.highlight: Some leaders make decisions after consulting the whole team. By contrast, others prefer to act quickly and accept personal responsibility for the result.

questions.prompt: Public rules are usually written down and clearly announced, whereas unwritten social expectations are often learned slowly through observation and experience.
questions.promptZh: 公開規則通常會被寫下並清楚公布，而沒有明文規定的社會期望往往需要透過觀察和經驗慢慢學習。
questions.answer: Public rules are usually written down and clearly announced. By contrast, unwritten social expectations are often learned slowly through observation and experience.
questions.answerZh: 公開規則通常會被寫下並清楚公布。相比之下，沒有明文規定的社會期望往往需要透過觀察和經驗慢慢學習。
questions.highlight: Public rules are usually written down and clearly announced. By contrast, unwritten social expectations are often learned slowly through observation and experience.
